name: aligned_transitive_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_aligned_transitive_external_body(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        0 < b,
        0 < c,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(b, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, c),
{
    aligned_transitive(a, b, c);
}
reason: `aligned_transitive` is `external_body` — divisibility transitivity is trusted without proof

name: aligned_transitive_auto_universal
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_transitive_auto_universal(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        0 < b,
        0 < c,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(b, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, c),
{
    aligned_transitive_auto();
}
reason: The auto lemma provides universal transitivity to the SMT solver — depends on the unverified `aligned_transitive` external_body

name: aligned_chain_three
reason: Two applications of the external_body axiom yield a 3-step transitivity chain — composes freely without verification

name: aligned_zero_any
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_zero_any(b: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(0, b),
{
}
reason: Zero is aligned to everything — tests the trivial base case of `aligned`

name: aligned_self
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_self(a: nat)
    requires
        a > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(a, a),
{
}
reason: Every nonzero value is self-aligned — tests the `a % a == 0` tautology
